user_message: Just came out of QBR preparation with Tailspin Toys. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 16:35 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Tailspin Toys

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 13:41 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 16:48 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Tailspin Toys

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-F trap (Skips threads older than recent (silent dropout)); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-F failure mode (Skips threads older than recent (silent dropout))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-F -- Skips threads older than recent (silent dropout)
